Client 2: Yeah, and I want it to feel… doable month to month, not just a spreadsheet that looks good.
Advisor: Totally. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start high level: roughly what’s the household gross income per year right now, all-in?
Client 1: All-in household gross is 169950 annually.
Advisor: Got it. And on the spending side, about what do your total monthly expenses run—everything living, bills, lifestyle, the usual?
Client 2: We’re around 8k most months—sorry, the exact number we use is 7800 per month.

Client 2: No—number of dependents is 0.
Advisor: Perfect. And then, just so you know how I work—my advisory channel is RIA (registered investment advisor). Does that line up with what you were expecting from today?
Client 2: I think so—RIA (registered investment advisor), yes.
Advisor: Great. And James, Mary—when you hear that, any immediate questions on how that differs from, say, a brokerage relationship?

Advisor: Okay—thanks, James. That all makes the picture pretty clean. Before we move into goals, I want to grab a couple household basics so I don’t assume wrong.
Advisor: First one: what state do you two file from—where are you residents?
Client 2: NJ.
Advisor: Got it—New Jersey. And marital status—just confirming, you’re married, right?
Client 1: Yes, married.

Advisor: First one: what state do you two file from—where are you residents?
Client 2: NJ.
Advisor: Got it—New Jersey. And marital status—just confirming, you’re married, right?
Client 1: Yes, married.
Advisor: Thanks. Any dependents we should plan around—kids, anyone financially dependent on you?

Advisor: Hi James, hi Mary—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you came in. Before we get into numbers, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. Mostly we want a clearer plan—investments, retirement timing, and just making sure we’re not missing anything obvious.
Client 2: Yeah, and I want it to feel… doable month to month, not just a spreadsheet that looks good.
Advisor: Totally. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start high level: roughly what’s the household gross income per year right now, all-in?
Client 1: All-in household gross is 169950 annually.

Client 2: I always mix this up—are you asking what we owe or the bracket?
Advisor: Bracket—so the percentage band you’re typically in, not the dollar amount you pay.
Client 1: Right—our tax bracket band is 32%.
Advisor: Perfect. And then risk—when markets swing, how would you describe your comfort level? Conservative, moderate, aggressive—what fits best?
Client 2: I’m not aggressive. I’d say moderate—like, we can handle normal ups and downs, just not anything wild.

Advisor: Hi James, hi Mary—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you came in. Before we get into numbers, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. Mostly we want a clearer plan—investments, retirement timing, and just making sure we’re not missing anything obvious.
Client 2: Yeah, and I want it to feel… doable month to month, not just a spreadsheet that looks good.
Advisor: Totally. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start high level: roughly what’s the household gross income per year right now, all-in?

Advisor: Bracket—so the percentage band you’re typically in, not the dollar amount you pay.
Client 1: Right—our tax bracket band is 32%.
Advisor: Perfect. And then risk—when markets swing, how would you describe your comfort level? Conservative, moderate, aggressive—what fits best?
Client 2: I’m not aggressive. I’d say moderate—like, we can handle normal ups and downs, just not anything wild.
Client 1: Yeah, moderate is the right word for us—risk tolerance is moderate.

Client 2: And keeping life comfortable. I don’t want us to feel like we’re pinching pennies just to hit some number.
Advisor: That makes sense. Before we map timelines, I want to anchor your income picture so the plan isn’t squishy. James—are you currently working, and what’s your gross annual income?
Client 1: Yes, I’m employed, and my gross annual income is 134000.
Advisor: Got it. And what do you do—what’s your role or general line of work?
Client 1: I’m in sales.

Client 2: And keeping life comfortable. I don’t want us to feel like we’re pinching pennies just to hit some number.
